_schema_notes: State-level defaults for South Carolina jurisdictions.
Values here are inherited by every SC jurisdiction config via deep-merge in load_config().
Per-jurisdiction values OVERRIDE these defaults when present.
Law-specific citations live in jurisdictions/_open_records_laws.json (loaded by audit.py) — the SC FOIA statute inventory is COMPLETE and verified there (2026-06-13).
This file holds non-law state defaults: body taxonomy, scraper hints, form-of-government norms.
PROVENANCE / STATUS (Tier 1 / Phase A, 2026-06-13): only structurally-safe defaults are populated.
Body aliases and records-custodian titles below reflect SC's statutory government structure
(Title 4 counties = 'County Council' + 'Clerk to Council'; Title 5 municipalities; school 'Board of
Trustees') and are CONVENTIONS to confirm in Phase C, not live-verified this session.
DELIBERATELY OMITTED — graduate in later phases, do NOT fabricate:
- election_cycle_defaults (term lengths, qualifying windows): Phase B. SC municipal elections
vary by charter (partisan/nonpartisan, odd/even-year) — no single default is honest yet.
- form_of_government default beyond the conservative stub: SC has multiple statutory forms
(Title 5 municipal: Mayor-Council / Council / Council-Manager; Title 4 county: Council /
Council-Administrator / Council-Manager / Council-Supervisor / Board of Commissioners). Phase C.
- platform_conventions (agenda/property platform typicals): Phase D platform census not yet run.
- SC does NOT use Clarity Elections (that is GA's certified-results vendor); SC results are
published by the SC Election Commission. Certified-results provider/URL to confirm in Phase D.

common_body_aliases._doc: Maps clerk-vocabulary variations to canonical body_type enum values. SC-specific: counties are governed by 'County Council' (Title 4); school districts by a 'Board of Trustees' (Title 59). Structural conventions — confirm in Phase C.

election_results_sources._doc: Where SC election results are published. SC does NOT use Clarity Elections (GA's vendor). The SC Election Commission publishes statewide results; per-county Boards of Voter Registration & Elections run local races. Certified-results base URL to confirm in Phase D.

ethics_filings._doc: SC campaign-finance disclosure is centralized at the State Ethics Commission (county and municipal candidates file there, not with a local clerk — S.C. Code § 8-13-100(2), § 8-13-365). Verified 2026-06-13.

records_custodian_conventions._doc: Default custodian title by jurisdiction type. SC counties statutorily have a 'Clerk to Council' (Title 4); municipalities a 'Municipal Clerk'/'City Clerk'. Names are always per-jurisdiction. Structural conventions — confirm in Phase C.
